source_file: The corporate social credit system in China and its transnational impact.txt
text:
3. The CSCS and its potential impact on corporations and
transnational corporate governance and international relations
Data-driven governance is not a Chinese invention and neither are its pro-
blematic aspects59 but the SCS, as envisioned, in its almost all-encompassing
scale is unprecedented.60 While the use of social media data and other data
points can be positive for some, for example when the lack of ﬁnancial
history has previously been a hindrance for credit,61 it can be a curse for
others as the use of correlation of diﬀerent data sets instead of causation
can lead to discriminatory practices.62 Many algorithmic assessment tools
operate with ﬂawed proxies that establish harmful correlations in order to
predict behaviour, trustworthiness and risk and thereby infringe rights.63
Yet, in the context of the global political economy and the role transnational
55
   See Cutler (n 8) 727 and Saskia Sassen, Territory, Authority, Rights: From Medieval to Global Assemblages
   (Princeton University Press, 2006).
56
   See Cutler (n 8) 728.
57
   Ibid.
58
   Bastiaan van Apeldoorn, ‘Theorizing the Transnational: A Historical Materialist Approach’ (2004) 7(2)
   Journal of International Relations and Development 142, 144 (original italics).
59
   Various forms of social credit systems with a less universal scope are used in Western societies. See
   Mac Sithigh and Siems (n 28) 1036–42.
60
   See Fei Shen (n 37) 21.
61
   See Botsman (n 22).
62
   See Mac Sithigh and Siems (n 28) 1043–5.
63
   The IEEE Global Initiative on Ethics of Autonomous and Intelligent Systems, ‘Ethically Aligned Design:
   A Vision for Prioritizing Human Well-being with Autonomous and Intelligent Systems’ First Edition,
   200. For more examples on ﬂawed algorithms in the United States see: Cathy O’Neil, Weapons of
   Math Destruction. How Big Data Increases Inequality and Threatens Democracy (Penguin Books,
   2017). Although mainly a work of advocacy, this book shows the human ﬂaws, corporate interests
   and logic misconceptions behind algorithmic tools and their (possibly adverse) impact on individuals
   and social justice.
                                                            TRANSNATIONAL LEGAL THEORY                   303
